text: Setting clear goals is essential for any project’s success. A useful approach is to set SMART goals, which means they are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," you could say "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." You then break this into milestones, such as finishing the design phase by the end of the first month. Each milestone leads to a deliverable, like a completed report or a new feature. Finally, stakeholder alignment ensures that everyone involved agrees on these goals, which helps avoid confusion and keeps the project on track.
